# UC-22.17.5 · Electronic records integrity — control theme 5

## Description

Supports Part 11 technical controls evidence by correlating validated system audit trails with identity and change records.

## Value

Supports Part 11 technical controls evidence for "Electronic records integrity — control theme 5" by correlating validated system audit trails with identity and change records.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=gxp`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=gxp sourcetype=lims:audit earliest=-400d
| eval uc_tag="22.17.5"
| eval retain_until=strptime(retention_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where retain_until < now() AND disposition!="destroyed"
| stats count by study_id, cabinet
```

Understanding this SPL

**LIMS records past retention without disposition (21 CFR Part 11)** — Supports Part 11 technical controls evidence by correlating validated system audit trails with identity and change records.

Documented **Data sources**: LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: gxp; **sourcetype**: lims:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=gxp, sourcetype=lims:audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **retain_until** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where retain_until < now() AND disposition!="destroyed"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by study_id, cabinet** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.



Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the source-of-truth system (LIMS, MES, or ELN) and compare with the audit register. Confirm users, instruments, and timestamps align with controlled quality records for the same activity.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value

## SPL

```spl
index=gxp sourcetype=lims:audit earliest=-400d
| eval uc_tag="22.17.5"
| eval retain_until=strptime(retention_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where retain_until < now() AND disposition!="destroyed"
| stats count by study_id, cabinet
```

## Visualization

Time chart, Table, Single value. Key fields: `cabinet`, `retain_until`, `study_id`, `uc_tag`.
